description:
You are a web-developer who has inherited a small JavaScript project living in 
  /home/user/webapp.  
  The project tree has already been prepared for you exactly as follows:

  /home/user/webapp
  ├─ assets/
  │  └─ js/
  │     ├─ script1.js
  │     └─ script2.js
  ├─ src/
  │  ├─ app.js
  │  └─ utils/
  │        └─ helper.js
  └─ README.md

  1. Your goal is to generate a single integrity manifest that lists **every**
     “*.js” file in the project (no other extensions) together with its
     MD5 checksum.

  2. The manifest must be written to
        /home/user/webapp/logs/js_checksums.log
     • If the directory /home/user/webapp/logs/ does not yet exist, create it.  
     • The file must be **overwritten** if it already exists.

  3. Format requirements for js_checksums.log (these WILL be verified):
     • Each record occupies exactly one line.  
     • A record is:

            <relative_path_from_/home/user/webapp><single_space><32-char_lowercase_hex_md5>

       Example of correct formatting (do **not** copy verbatim):
            assets/js/file.js d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e

     • The relative paths must
       – use forward slashes (/),  
       – NOT begin with “./”,  
       – be sorted in normal lexicographical order (as produced by the POSIX
         sort(1) utility).

  4. Only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities are allowed.  Achieve the
     entire job with a single pipeline that combines find(1) and xargs(1)
     (additional tools like md5sum, sort, mkdir etc. may appear in the
     pipeline).

  5. When you are done, display the resulting js_checksums.log on the console
     so the user can visually confirm its contents.

  Success criteria checked by the automated test:
     • /home/user/webapp/logs/js_checksums.log exists.
     • It contains exactly four (4) non-empty lines, one per *.js file.
     • Lines are in ascending lexicographical order by path.
     • The left field of each line matches the set
         {assets/js/script1.js, assets/js/script2.js,
          src/app.js, src/utils/helper.js}.
     • The right field of each line is a valid MD5 sum that **exactly**
       matches the file’s current content.
truth:
Initial file structure and file contents present **before** the agent starts:

  1. /home/user/webapp/assets/js/script1.js
     Content (including trailing newline):
       console.log('Script One');\n

  2. /home/user/webapp/assets/js/script2.js
     Content:
       console.log('Script Two');\n

  3. /home/user/webapp/src/app.js
     Content:
       console.log('Main App');\n

  4. /home/user/webapp/src/utils/helper.js
     Content:
       export function greet() { return 'Hello'; }\n

  5. /home/user/webapp/README.md
     Content:
       # Sample project\n

  The automated test will:

  • Re-compute the MD5 checksum of each file above.  
  • Verify that /home/user/webapp/logs/js_checksums.log exists and is readable.  
  • Read the file, split each non-empty line at the first space, and build a
    mapping {relative_path → md5}.  
  • Confirm that the file contains EXACTLY these four keys and that each
    reported checksum matches the recomputed value.  
  • Confirm that the 4 lines appear in lexicographical order (Python
    sorted(list_of_keys) must equal the order read from the file).

  Any deviation will fail the task.
name: task_000000_a3fed0bd
